사도행전 19:1.content: While Apollos was at Corinth, Paul, having passed through the upper country, came to Ephesus and found certain disciples.

사도행전 19:2.content: He said to them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?” They said to him, “No, we haven’t even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”

사도행전 19:3.content: He said, “Into what then were you baptized?” They said, “Into John’s baptism.”

사도행전 19:4.content: Paul said, “John indeed baptized with the baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe in the one who would come after him, that is, in Jesus.”

사도행전 19:5.content: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.

사도행전 19:6.content: When Paul had laid his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came on them and they spoke with other languages and prophesied.

사도행전 19:7.content: They were about twelve men in all.

사도행전 19:8.content: He entered into the synagogue and spoke boldly for a period of three months, reasoning and persuading about the things concerning God’s Kingdom.

사도행전 19:9.content: But when some were hardened and disobedient, speaking evil of the Way before the multitude, he departed from them, and separated the disciples, reasoning daily in the school of Tyrannus.

사도행전 19:10.content: This continued for two years, so that all those who lived in Asia heard the word of the Lord Jesus, both Jews and Greeks.

사도행전 19:11.content: God worked special miracles by the hands of Paul,

사도행전 19:12.content: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons were carried away from his body to the sick, and the diseases departed from them, and the evil spirits went out.

사도행전 19:13.content: But some of the itinerant Jews, exorcists, took on themselves to invoke over those who had the evil spirits the name of the Lord Jesus, saying, “We adjure you by Jesus whom Paul preaches.”

사도행전 19:14.content: There were seven sons of one Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, who did this.

사도행전 19:15.content: The evil spirit answered, “Jesus I know, and Paul I know, but who are you?”

사도행전 19:16.content: The man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, overpowered them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.

사도행전 19:17.content: This became known to all, both Jews and Greeks, who lived at Ephesus. Fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was magnified.

사도행전 19:18.content: Many also of those who had believed came, confessing, and declaring their deeds.

사도행전 19:19.content: Many of those who practiced magical arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all. They counted their price, and found it to be fifty thousand pieces of silver.

사도행전 19:20.content: So the word of the Lord was growing and becoming mighty.

사도행전 19:21.content: Now after these things had ended, Paul determined in the Spirit, when he had passed through Macedonia and Achaia, to go to Jerusalem, saying, “After I have been there, I must also see Rome.”

사도행전 19:22.content: Having sent into Macedonia two of those who served him, Timothy and Erastus, he himself stayed in Asia for a while.

사도행전 19:23.content: About that time there arose no small disturbance concerning the Way.

사도행전 19:24.content: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen,

사도행전 19:25.content: whom he gathered together, with the workmen of like occupation, and said, “Sirs, you know that by this business we have our wealth.

사도행전 19:26.content: You see and hear that not at Ephesus alone, but almost throughout all Asia, this Paul has persuaded and turned away many people, saying that they are no gods that are made with hands.

사도행전 19:27.content: Not only is there danger that this our trade come into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be counted as nothing and her majesty destroyed, whom all Asia and the world worships.”

사도행전 19:28.content: When they heard this they were filled with anger, and cried out, saying, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

사도행전 19:29.content: The whole city was filled with confusion, and they rushed with one accord into the theater, having seized Gaius and Aristarchus, men of Macedonia, Paul’s companions in travel.

사도행전 19:30.content: When Paul wanted to enter in to the people, the disciples didn’t allow him.

사도행전 19:31.content: Certain also of the Asiarchs, being his friends, sent to him and begged him not to venture into the theater.

사도행전 19:32.content: Some therefore cried one thing, and some another, for the assembly was in confusion. Most of them didn’t know why they had come together.

사도행전 19:33.content: They brought Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. Alexander beckoned with his hand, and would have made a defense to the people.

사도행전 19:34.content: But when they perceived that he was a Jew, all with one voice for a time of about two hours cried out, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”

사도행전 19:35.content: When the town clerk had quieted the multitude, he said, “You men of Ephesus, what man is there who doesn’t know that the city of the Ephesians is temple keeper of the great goddess Artemis, and of the image which fell down from Zeus?

사도행전 19:36.content: Seeing then that these things can’t be denied, you ought to be quiet and to do nothing rash.

사도행전 19:37.content: For you have brought these men here, who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of your goddess.

사도행전 19:38.content: If therefore Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a matter against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls. Let them press charges against one another.

사도행전 19:39.content: But if you seek anything about other matters, it will be settled in the regular assembly.

사도행전 19:40.content: For indeed we are in danger of being accused concerning today’s riot, there being no cause. Concerning it, we wouldn’t be able to give an account of this commotion.”

사도행전 19:41.content: When he had thus spoken, he dismissed the assembly.
